messages.content:
CipherVault encrypts files with **AES-GCM** using a key derived from your **master password**.

The flow is:

- Your password is turned into an encryption key with **`PBKDF2WithHmacSHA256`** using a **random salt** and **310,000 iterations**:  
  “uses `PBKDF2WithHmacSHA256` with a random salt to turn your password into an encryption key” (README.md) [claim:01KR7M5M5WVFZ8YQMM0Z51JGZ7]  
  “your master password is hashed with `PBKDF2WithHmacSHA256` (random salt, 310 000 iterations) … The same password is used to derive the AES key.” (README.md)

- When you import a file, CipherVault encrypts it **before it touches disk**:  
  “every file you import is encrypted with `AES/GCM/NoPadding` before it touches disk” (README.md)  
  “On import — the file is read into memory, encrypted with AES-256-GCM (random IV), and written to `~/.ciphervault/vault/`. Only the ciphertext ever touches disk.” (README.md) [claim:01KR7M5M5WJ382N2NKJTAC961M]

- In code, `CryptoService` does this with Java’s AES-GCM cipher and a fresh random IV each time:  
  `Cipher.getInstance("AES/GCM/NoPadding");` `src/main/java/com/ciphervault/security/CryptoService.java:18`  
  `secureRandom.nextBytes(iv);` `src/main/java/com/ciphervault/security/CryptoService.java:16`  
  `new GCMParameterSpec(TAG_LENGTH_BITS, iv)` `src/main/java/com/ciphervault/security/CryptoService.java:19`  
  That’s the implementation behind “CryptoService.encrypt uses AES/GCM/NoPadding.” [claim:01KR7JVCSVGY3FGE4ETNWDT5YY]

- It uses:
  - **12-byte IVs**: `private static final int IV_LENGTH_BYTES = 12;` `src/main/java/com/ciphervault/security/CryptoService.java:9`
  - **128-bit authentication tags**: `private static final int TAG_LENGTH_BITS = 128;` `src/main/java/com/ciphervault/security/CryptoService.java:10`

- On decrypt/export, it reverses the process in memory:  
  `Cipher.getInstance("AES/GCM/NoPadding");` `src/main/java/com/ciphervault/security/CryptoService.java:29`  
  `cipher.doFinal(payload.ciphertext());` `src/main/java/com/ciphervault/security/CryptoService.java:34`  
  matching “On export, the encrypted blob is decrypted in memory” (README.md).

One extra detail from the code: CipherVault also supports **associated authenticated data (AAD)** during encryption/decryption via `cipher.updateAAD(associatedData);` `src/main/java/com/ciphervault/security/CryptoService.java:21` and `src/main/java/com/ciphervault/security/CryptoService.java:32`. That means it can authenticate extra metadata along with the ciphertext.

So, short version: **password-derived AES key + random salt for derivation + random IV per file + AES/GCM authenticated encryption + only ciphertext written to disk**.
